name: tastopo

metadata.name: tastopo
metadata.summary: Generate printable topographic maps for anywhere in Tasmania

documentation:
# TasTopo

*Generate printable topographic maps for anywhere in Tasmania.*

The Tasmanian Government's [ListMap service](https://maps.thelist.tas.gov.au) provides an online map viewer that can display a wealth of publicly accessible geospatial data covering the whole of Tasmania. It allows users to generate maps as printable PDFs, but lacks fine-grained controls.

TasTopo is a command-line tool that provides an alternative way to create printable maps using ListMap data. It uses the ListMap ArcGIS API to fetch an image of the map area required, before wrapping it in a minimal SVG template and exporting it as a PDF file.

![Example map](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jonathanholvey/tastopo/main/example.svg)

## Features

- Location searchable by name or geo URI
- Continuously variable scale ratio
- Dynamic sheet sizing from A5 to A0
- Landscape or portrait orientation
- Minimal design to maximise map area
- SVG and PDF export formats
- Enhanced hillshade overlay

## Installation

TasTopo requires Python 3 and can be installed using Pip:

```bash
python3 -m pip install tastopo
```

## Usage

```
tastopo generate [options] <location>
```

To generate a map, provide either the name of a place, or its coordinates as a [geo URI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geo_URI_scheme) for the `<location>` argument, as well as any options required. Valid locations include:

- `kettering`
- `'kunyani / Mount Wellington'`
- `'geo:-43.643611,146.8275'`

### Layout options

- `--scale`: Specify the scale of the printed map (defaults to 25000)
- `--title`: Set the title on the map sheet, instead of the location name
- `--paper`: Specify the paper size for printing (defaults to A4)
- `--portrait`: Orientate the map in portrait, rather than landscape

For details of all the available options run `tastopo --help`.

### Example

A PDF version of the the map image shown above can be generated using the following command:

```bash
tastopo generate --paper a5 --scale 28000 kettering
```

## License and copyright

TasTopo is released under the [MIT license](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jonathanholvey/tastopo/main/LICENSE.txt).

Mapping data is sourced from Land Tasmania's ListMap service, under the Creative Commons 3.0 Australia license. Topographic and hillshade grey map layers from www.thelist.tas.gov.au © State of Tasmania.

functions.name: tastopo.validate.validate
documentation:
Validate CLI arguments and raise exception on invalid input

```
param args

The parsed CLI arguments provided by docopt

returns

Nothing
```

\:raises ValueError

functions.name: tastopo.export.clean_filename
documentation:
Remove invalid characters from a filename

```
param filename

The original file name for cleaning

returns

The sanitised file name
```

functions.name: tastopo.export.export_map
documentation:
Export a map document

```
param svg

The map SVG root element to be exported

param filetype

The file type/extension to export the map SVG as

param filename

The name of the output file, with or without an extension
```

functions.name: tastopo.image.frombytes
documentation:
Create an image object from a PNG byte array

```
param data

The PNG byte data to parse

returns

A PIL image object
```

functions.name: tastopo.image.tobytes
documentation:
Convert an image object to a PNG byte array

```
param image

A PIL image object to export

returns

An PNG byte array
```

functions.name: tastopo.image.stitch
documentation:
Join an array of image tiles into a single image

```
param tiles

A list of tiles to stitch

param size

The size of the grid to stitch the tiles into

param start

The position in the grid of the first tile

returns

A single image made up of all the tiles
```

functions.name: tastopo.image.layer
documentation:
Merge multiple image layers together

```
param background

The image to use as the base layer

param layers

Additional images to layer on top, with opacity in 0-1 range

returns

The layers combined in a single image
```

Create a sheet of paper

```
The name of a paper size from the ISO-206 A series
```

documentation:
Get the width and height of the paper size being represented

:returns: The width and height in
A piece of paper from the ISO-206 A series

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.__init__
documentation:
Create a new tile grid

```
param layer

The map layer to the grid is for

param level

The LOD level of the grid image

param centre

The coordinates of the centre of the grid

param size

The width and height of the grid
```

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.tiles
documentation:
Get a list of tile coordinates to cover a real-world map area

:returns: A list of grid tile coordinates

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.grid
documentation:
Get the grid definition

:returns: The coordinates of the start of the grid, and its size

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.bbox
documentation:
Get the coordinates of the corners bounding the map area

:returns: The coordinates of the top left and bottom right corners of the grid

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.tileunits
documentation:
Convert a real-world distance in metres to a number of tile widths

```
param size

The dimension to be converted

returns

The converted value in metres
```

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.pixelsize
documentation:
Get the grid dimensions in pixels

:returns: The size of the grid in pixels

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.overflow
documentation:
Get the proportion of a tile that the grid extends beyond the map area by on each side

:returns: The overflow amount on each side, grouped by horizontal and vertical dimensions

methods.name: tastopo.dimensions.tilegrid.TileGrid.origin
documentation:
Get the pixel position of the first tile, relative to the start of the map area

:returns: The position of the first tile, in pixels
Calculate the dimensions of a grid of map tiles

methods.name: tastopo.map.Sheet.__init__
documentation:
Create a map sheet

```
param size

The paper size to use

param rotated

Set the orientation of the sheet; portrait when True, landscape otherwise
```

methods.name: tastopo.map.Sheet.dimensions
documentation:
Get the sheet dimensions while considering the sheet orientation.

Landscape is used when self.rotated is not True
:returns: The width and height in mm

methods.name: tastopo.map.Sheet.imagesize
documentation:
Get the size of the map image for the selected paper size

:returns: The width and height of the image in mm

methods.name: tastopo.map.Sheet.viewport
documentation:
Get the dimensions and position of the map image for the selected paper size

```
param with_bleed

When True, the dimensions include extra width and height so the image overlaps the viewport

returns

The width, height, and x and y offsets in mm
```

functions.name: tastopo.mapdata.geometry.centroid
documentation:
Calculate the centroid of a polygon defined by a list of vertex coordinates.

Formula from Bourke (1997) <http://paulbourke.net/geometry/polygonmesh>

```
param points

A list of points that describe the outline of a polygon

returns

The x and y position of the polygon's centre
```
